they_believe: It was raining when she saw Mum crying in the car at Bright Horizons. She can see the rain on the window, the wet car park, the wipers not moving because the engine was off.
actually: It wasn't raining. The memory has fused with other drop-offs, other grey mornings, and the rain is a detail her brain added to make the scene feel as heavy as the emotion. Several drop-offs happened in rain. This specific one didn't.

they_believe: The stone was blue-black and smooth and fit perfectly in her hand, like it was made for her. She remembers the exact color, the exact texture, the exact shape.
actually: The stone was probably just a regular grey piece of gravel from the driveway crack. Four-year-olds don't have reliable color memory, and the stone has been elevated by grief into something more precious and specific than it was. The 'blue-black' is almost certainly a confabulation — there are no blue-black stones native to that driveway, just standard London gravel.

they_believe: The radiator knocked every single evening without fail, at exactly the same time, like clockwork. She's certain of the regularity because the regularity was what made it scary — the reliability of the fear.
actually: The radiator knocked when the heating kicked on, which depended on the thermostat, the boiler cycle, and whether Klára had turned it up. It probably knocked most evenings but not every one, and not at the same time. Anna's brain has compressed the pattern into something more absolute than it was because absolute patterns feel more controllable than random ones, even when the pattern is threatening.

they_believe: She was definitely five, maybe six, when she found out 'proboha' was Czech. She remembers being small enough that the embarrassment was physical, a full-body flush.
actually: She was probably closer to four. The embarrassment has aged the memory — it feels bigger, so she assumes she was bigger. But Markéta Dvořáková stopped coming around regularly when Anna was about four and a half, and the interaction likely happened earlier than Anna places it.
